page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dlsv7 family. It is equipped with 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system features 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on remote Premium Storage capabilities for disk operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in compute-intensive tasks. Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics, rank in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier performance in the top 10%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falls in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is in the top 25%, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50%.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D48ls_v7 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage. This configuration provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large in-memory datasets or local scratch disks. It leverages advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX to accelerate matrix and vector operations, which is reflected in its strong performance during small and medium LLM inference benchmarks.

This server is suited for compute-optimized workloads, including web serving, data decompression pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference. Its high clock speed and dedicated core allocation support multi-threaded processing tasks, static web serving, and database operations that fit within the 96.0 GB memory envelope.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-oriented x86_64 server from the Dlsv7 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, representing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in CPU-bound tasks, with Passmark CPU scores, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks reach the top 10%. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory footprints or local storage. It is suited for web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong performance in CPU-bound benchmarks and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring forty-eight dedicated Intel vCPUs and a low-memory profile for high-throughput processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage capabilities
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference and static web serving
